title: calea neblinensis
intro: Calea neblinensis, also known as the Amazon water lily, is a plant species native to the Amazon rainforest. It has been traditionally used in medicine for its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones. The medicinal properties of Calea neblinensis have been found to provide health benefits such as reducing pain and inflammation, and it is often used to treat conditions like arthritis and other musculoskeletal disorders. In herbal preparations, Calea neblinensis is typically dried and processed into capsules, tablets, or teas, and can also be applied topically in the form of creams or ointments.
